bio.photo_notes: Cloudinary; supplied by Sam — Asia Society Japan Center keynote photo

sources.short: Asia Society Japan Center launch keynote (https://asiasociety.org)

narrative_blocks.header: Fourth Rockefeller generation in Karuizawa
narrative_blocks.body: His grandfather opened the International House of Japan in 1955; his father studied three years there afterward. Charles continues the family thread as Asia Society trustee.

bio.photo_notes: Cloudinary; supplied by Sam — Asia Society Japan Center keynote photo

sources.short: Asia Society Japan Center launch keynote (https://asiasociety.org)

narrative_blocks.header: Fourth Rockefeller generation in Karuizawa
narrative_blocks.body: His grandfather opened the International House of Japan in 1955; his father studied three years there afterward. Charles continues the family thread as Asia Society trustee.

bio.title: Sen.
bio.extra_names: Commonly 'Jay Rockefeller'; full name John Davison Rockefeller IV

birth.historical_notes: Born at New York Hospital Manhattan 26 days after the death of his patrilineal great-grandfather John D. Rockefeller Sr. (d. May 23, 1937).

education.notes: A.B. in Far Eastern languages and history.
Three-year interregnum mid-Harvard studying Japanese; followed by 6 months in Kyoto with painter Uemura Shoko. No INST entry — only one Hooker-tree affiliation.
Non-degree graduate work in Oriental studies; studied Chinese.

careers.notes: VISTA worker in Emmons West Virginia under President Lyndon B. Johnson. The move that began his West Virginia public-service career — Rockefeller scion entered Appalachian politics by living + working in coal-mining poverty country.

careers.employer: West Virginia House of Delegates (Kanawha County)
careers.notes: First elected office. Rejected uncle Nelson Rockefeller's offer of an open NY Senate seat after RFK's 1968 assassination, choosing to stay in West Virginia.
22nd Secretary of State of WV under Governor Arch A. Moore Jr. Won 1968 election with 60.92% of vote.
After losing 1972 gubernatorial race to Arch Moore, served as college president 1973-1975.

29th Governor of West Virginia. Two terms 1977-1985. Inaugurated Jan 17, 1977. Period of severe WV recession 1982-1984 (15-20% unemployment) due to coal + manufacturing decline. Brought 20+ Japanese corporations to WV including Toyota plant — leveraging his Asian fluency for state economic development.

30 years in US Senate. Authored Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP) 1997 — covers ~6 million children annually. Major role in Affordable Care Act 2010 passage; advocated public option. Multiple terms Veterans' Affairs Committee chair. Senate Finance Committee. Senate Intelligence Committee chair (until 2009). Commerce Committee chair (2009-2015). Senior US Senator from WV after Robert Byrd's 2010 death. The ONLY member of the Rockefeller family to hold elected office as a Democrat (in a traditionally Republican family). Did not seek reelection 2014; succeeded by Republican Shelley Moore Capito.

Co-founded with mother Blanchette in 1999 — named in her honor as her Alzheimer's diagnosis prompted her to support memory research. $30M state-of-the-art research facility opened at WVU Fall 2008 designed for 100 scientists.

narrative_blocks.header: Moved to a West Virginia coal town
narrative_blocks.body: Born Manhattan to America's wealthiest family, he relocated to Emmons WV as a 1964 VISTA volunteer. He stayed, becoming the only Democratic Rockefeller to win statewide office.

narrative_blocks.header: Refused his uncle's offered Senate seat
narrative_blocks.body: In 1968 his uncle Nelson offered him Robert Kennedy's vacated New York seat. Jay declined and stayed on the West Virginia path.

narrative_blocks.header: Six months apprenticed to painter Uemura Shoko
narrative_blocks.body: Three years at International Christian University Tokyo, then six months in Kyoto with the master. Japanese fluency he later turned into West Virginia jobs.

narrative_blocks.header: Authored health insurance for six million children
narrative_blocks.body: The Children's Health Insurance Program passed in 1997 with bipartisan support. It still covers six million American children annually.

cross_connections.display_label: his father-in-law, a four-term U.S. senator

institutions.institution_blurb: Graduated 1955; same New Hampshire school where C. Osgood Hooker (H04265) studied in the 1870s.
A.B. 1961 in Far Eastern languages and history; took three years off mid-degree to study at ICU Tokyo.
Graduate work in Oriental studies; non-degree, studied Chinese language.

parents.mother_research_notes: Jeanne Valerie Dickerson Percy (X02343, d.1947 when Sharon was 3). Stepmother Loraine Diane Guyer Percy (X02344, m. Charles 1950, d.2020) raised Sharon from age 6+.

careers.notes: During Jay's two gubernatorial terms. Founded Mountain Artisans (quilting business for low-income WV artisans); promoted PBS; established centralized system to assist mentally disabled children.

Since 1989 — over 35 years tenure. WETA broadcasts PBS NewsHour, Washington Week, Ken Burns documentaries. Convinced Burns to produce 'Cancer: The Emperor of All Maladies' (2015) after her own colon cancer diagnosis 2005.
Multi-year board member of CPB; eventually chairwoman. Five terms PBS Board of Directors.

notable.notable_blurb: WETA-TV CEO 1989-present; National Medal of Arts 2019

narrative_blocks.header: Twin sister murdered as Charles ran for Senate
narrative_blocks.body: Valerie was beaten and stabbed in her bedroom on Sept 18, 1966 — six weeks before their father won the Senate seat. Sharon later named her own daughter Valerie Blanchette.

narrative_blocks.header: Persuaded Ken Burns to film cancer documentary
narrative_blocks.body: After her own 2005 colon cancer diagnosis she pressed the filmmaker. The PBS documentary aired in 2015.

narrative_blocks.header: Took Trump's arts medal in 2019
narrative_blocks.body: WETA had broadcast PBS NewsHour through years of his network attacks. She accepted the National Medal of Arts that November anyway.

cross_connections.display_label: First Lady of Massachusetts and a China-law scholar

death.cause: Automobile accident (Tarrytown NY area en route to Pocantico Hills)

parents.father_research_notes: John D. Rockefeller Jr. (1874-1960) — son of John D. Rockefeller Sr. (Standard Oil founder).
parents.mother_research_notes: Abby Aldrich Rockefeller (1874-1948) — co-founder Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) 1929; daughter of Senator Nelson W. Aldrich RI.

marriages.notes: Per Wikipedia + Findagrave: m. Nov 11, 1932 Riverside Church NYC. (Vassar Encyclopedia + earlier Hooker tree had Nov 19 — Wikipedia says Nov 11 — going with Wikipedia primary biography.) Met Blanchette over tennis + horse-riding 1931, less than a year before wedding. 4 children: Sandra (1935-2024), John D. 'Jay' Rockefeller IV (b.1937 — US Senator + WV Governor), Hope Aldrich (b.1938 — newspaper publisher), Alida Ferry (b.1949). Children built per Sam's expanded scope for the notable Rockefeller branch.

sources.short: Rockefeller Archive Center — JDR 3rd papers + Blanchette H. Rockefeller papers

notable.notable_blurb: Asia Society + Lincoln Center founder; Rockefeller philanthropist

narrative_blocks.header: Killed in a car accident near Pocantico
narrative_blocks.body: July 10, 1978, age 72, on the road to the family estate. Princeton named Rockefeller College after him four years later.

narrative_blocks.header: Founded Asia Society in 1956
narrative_blocks.body: After accompanying John Foster Dulles to Tokyo for the 1951 Peace Treaty, he committed to closer U.S.-Asia ties. The Society now has eleven international centers.

narrative_blocks.header: Went to Tokyo with John Foster Dulles
narrative_blocks.body: Dulles took him as a personal advisor for the 1951 Peace Treaty negotiations. The trip launched the Asia Society, Japan Society, and Population Council.

narrative_blocks.header: Cleared seventeen acres of tenements for Lincoln Center
narrative_blocks.body: He chaired the Lincoln Center building committee from 1956. The complex displaced seven thousand residents from San Juan Hill.

burial.plot_notes: Rockefeller Family Cemetery, Sleepy Hollow Westchester Co NY

education.notes: Preparatory school Windsor CT, graduated 1925. Note: Loomis Chaffee is in Windsor CT — the same Windsor where Hooker forebear Horace Hooker (H00798) lived before migrating to Rochester NY 1821. Geographic coincidence in his preparatory education + future wife's Hooker ancestry.

Per Wikipedia: graduated 1929 with high honors in economics. Senior thesis on industrial relations — chosen 'because of family's role in the Ludlow Massacre' (1914 strike-breaking incident at Rockefeller-controlled Colorado Fuel and Iron Company that killed women + children of striking miners). Father JDR Jr. spent decades repairing the family's reputation after Ludlow.

careers.notes: Founded 1956 to foster cooperation between Asia + the United States. JDR III had been deeply invested in Asia since his accompanying Secretary of State John Foster Dulles + Douglas MacArthur to Japan in late 1950 to conclude a peace treaty. He consulted with Japanese leaders on virtually every aspect of Japanese postwar life.
Founded 1952. Major supporter of population studies + family planning research. Chaired the Commission on Population Growth + American Future 1970-1972.

careers.employer: Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts
careers.notes: Mid-1950s to 1970: assumed leadership of the Exploratory Committee for a Musical Arts Center → became key fund-raiser → second president of Lincoln Center 1956-1961 → chairman 1961-1970 → honorary chairman thereafter. The Center itself was built 1959-1969.
From the Fund's inception 1940 through 1956. Rockefeller family principal philanthropy run by family members.
Joined 1929; elected to board 1931; subsequently chairman for 20 years. Responsible for changing the institution's focus.

careers.employer: JDR III Fund + Asian Cultural Council
careers.notes: Founded the JDR III Fund 1963. Major program: Asian Cultural Program 1967, encouraging East-West cultural exchange. Continued after his death 1979 as the Asian Cultural Council. Has provided grant assistance to 4,000+ Asians + Americans in arts.

bio.extra_names: Mrs. John D. Rockefeller III; Blanchette H. Rockefeller

death.cause: Pneumonia, a complication of Alzheimer's disease
death.note: Per Wikipedia + Findagrave: died at her home near Briarcliff Manor NY of pneumonia complicating Alzheimer's. Findagrave alternately lists 'New York, NY' as place of death — both refer to the Briarcliff Manor / Pocantico area.

marriages.notes: 4 children with John D. Rockefeller III: Sandra (b.1935), John D. 'Jay' Rockefeller IV (b.1937, US Senator from West Virginia 1985-2015), Hope (b.1938), Alida (b.1949). Children intentionally not built per Hooker-centric scope — they would be gen 10 via Hooker (compiler is gen 12). Jay Rockefeller is still living (b.1937).

education.notes: Per Wikipedia + Vassar Encyclopedia: graduated Vassar 1931 with A.B. in music. Class president. 'Most respected' and 'most perfect lady' per Vassar yearbook senior poll. Wrote senior paper 'The Individual in America' on freedom requiring economic security — a foundational philosophical statement informing her later philanthropy. Music professor George Sherman Dickinson attended her 1932 wedding.

careers.notes: First MoMA presidency.

Second MoMA presidency. Drove the decade-long fundraising campaign that doubled MoMA's exhibition space + funded the adjoining residential tower whose income supports MoMA operations + acquisitions. Per architect Cesar Pelli (consulting on the expansion): 'Blanchette was the hero of this whole process as far as I'm concerned.'
Per Findagrave: founding chairman of MoMA's Junior Council. The Junior Council was an early MoMA initiative to engage younger collectors + arts patrons.

Per Vassar Encyclopedia article: 8-year term as trustee 1948-1956. Continued donations + close correspondence with Vassar presidents (especially Alan Simpson) for the rest of her life. Donated extensive Asian + modern art (Rothko, Klee, Miro, Matisse) to Vassar's art gallery; 1981 50th-class-reunion booklet exclusively documented her donations.

careers.employer: Community Service Society of New York City
careers.notes: Per Vassar Encyclopedia: first FEMALE director of CSS NYC, 1933 — at age 24, just months after marrying JDR III.

sources.short: Rockefeller Archive Center — Blanchette H. Rockefeller papers (Series 53/1: Hooker and Ferry Family Files)
RAC — 'Blanchette Hooker Rockefeller: Muse of the Museum of Modern Art' research report
Findagrave: Blanchette Ferry Hooker Rockefeller (Rockefeller Family Cemetery)

narrative_blocks.header: Senior class voted her 'most perfect lady'
narrative_blocks.body: Vassar 1931 also named her senior class president. Music professor George Sherman Dickinson attended her wedding the next year.

narrative_blocks.header: Built a Philip Johnson art salon
narrative_blocks.body: In 1948 she commissioned the modernist Rockefeller Guest House at 242 East 52nd Street to display her growing modern art collection. She gave the house to MoMA in 1955.

narrative_blocks.header: Senior thesis defended economic security
narrative_blocks.body: Her 1931 Vassar paper argued freedom required financial security as a precondition. She lived the argument as the first female director of the Community Service Society of New York at twenty-four.

narrative_blocks.header: Brain institute named for her after death
narrative_blocks.body: She died of Alzheimer's complications in 1992. Jay co-founded the Blanchette Rockefeller Neurosciences Institute seven years later as a tribute.

cross_connections.display_label: her father-in-law, who built Rockefeller Center

burial.plot_notes: Rockefeller Family Cemetery, Sleepy Hollow Westchester Co NY

bio.title: Senator
bio.extra_names: Known as 'Chuck Percy.' Surname descended from the powerful Herndon family of Virginia + colonial-era Mississippians via paternal line.
bio.photo_notes: Cloudinary; supplied by Sam — official 1977 Senate portrait

death.note: Died at the Washington Home and Community Hospice in Washington DC, age 91.

parents.father_research_notes: Edward H. Percy — Alabama native, descended from illustrious colonial-era Mississippians + Virginians via the powerful Herndon family of Virginia. Variously automobile salesman + bank cashier. Son of Charles Brown Percy + Helen Leila Herndon. Not built per Hooker-centric scope.
parents.mother_research_notes: Elizabeth Harting — Illinois-born concert violinist. Daughter of Phineas Fredrick Harting + Belle Aschenbach. Source of Charles's middle name. Not built per Hooker-centric scope.

marriages.notes: First wife Jeanne d.1947. Three biological children: twin daughters Valerie + Sharon (b.1944) + son Roger (b.1946). Only Sharon built per Hooker scope (she is the Hooker-tree spouse via her 1967 marriage to Jay Rockefeller); siblings Valerie + Roger are not built per Jalapeño Rule (siblings of a Hooker spouse fall outside scope).

Second wife Loraine, m.1950. Two biological children Gail Percy (b.1953) + Mark C. Percy (b.1955); neither built per Jalapeño Rule (half-siblings of Hooker spouse Sharon, outside scope). Loraine became step-mother to Charles's three children from Jeanne after their 1950 marriage.

education.notes: Per Wikipedia. Public schools in Chicago + Winnetka.

On half-tuition scholarship; worked through college at multiple part-time jobs. Member of Alpha Delta Phi fraternity. His future Senate opponent Paul Douglas was his economics professor. The Charles H. Percy Summer in Washington Program at U Chicago Institute of Politics is named in his honor + funded by daughter Sharon Percy Rockefeller's $250,000 gift.

military_service.notes: Enlisted as apprentice seaman 1943; honorably discharged 1945 with rank of Lieutenant. Per Wikipedia.

career.notes: Started 1938 while still in college. Got the position via his Sunday school teacher Joseph McNabb (then Bell & Howell president, who took notice of Percy's pluck in mid-1930s).
Within a year of returning full-time post-graduation, Percy was appointed a director — extraordinary for a 22-year-old.

career.role: President + CEO + Chairman of the Board
career.notes: Promoted to president after Joseph McNabb's 1949 death. Led the company through 32-fold revenue growth + 12-fold employee growth. Listed Bell & Howell on the New York Stock Exchange. Diversified the company from movie cameras + projectors into microfilm + information services. 1949: Jaycees named Percy one of the 'Outstanding Young Men in America' alongside future U.S. President Gerald R. Ford Jr.
Appointed by President Eisenhower as personal representative to presidential inaugurations in Peru + Bolivia with rank of special ambassador, 1956.
Lost to Democratic incumbent Otto Kerner. Reluctantly endorsed Republican presidential nominee Barry Goldwater during the campaign.

Elected 1966 defeating Democratic incumbent Paul Douglas (his former U Chicago economics professor) 56-44. Re-elected 1972 (landslide vs Roman Pucinski) + 1978 (53-46 vs Alex Seith after famous tearful TV plea: 'I got your message and you're right'). Defeated 1984 by Democrat Paul Simon. Belonged to moderate/liberal 'Rockefeller Republican' wing of GOP led by Eisenhower + later Nelson Rockefeller. Mentioned as Republican presidential prospect 1968-1988. President Lyndon Johnson privately said in 1967 Percy 'would make a fine president.' Voted for Civil Rights Act of 1968 + Thurgood Marshall confirmation; voted against Haynsworth + Carswell Supreme Court nominations; voted for Rehnquist 1971. After Percy's defeat, no Republican won an Illinois Senate seat until Peter Fitzgerald 1998; no Republican has won Percy's old seat since.

Chair after Republicans took Senate control 1980. 1980: hosted luncheon for the 16th Karmapa of Tibet at the U.S. Capitol. Declined to sign 1975 'AIPAC Letter of 76' protesting President Ford's Middle East policy reassessment; called PLO leader Yasser Arafat 'moderate' relative to other Palestinians. Mearsheimer + Walt (2006) attributed his 1984 defeat to AIPAC's campaign against him.
Co-founded with Sen. Hubert H. Humphrey 1977 in response to 1973 OPEC oil embargo. National-energy-efficiency advocacy organization.
Post-Senate consulting business. Continued service on multiple foundation + committee boards.

sources.short: Biographical Directory of the United States Congress (Charles Harting Percy)
University of Chicago Magazine: 'Charles H. Percy Summer in Washington Program'

narrative_blocks.header: Sunday school teacher gave him his first job
narrative_blocks.body: His Sunday school teacher Joseph McNabb — president of Bell & Howell — noticed his pluck in the mid-1930s. By 1949 Percy himself was running the company; under his leadership it grew 32-fold and listed on the NYSE.

narrative_blocks.header: Foreign Relations Chair 1981-1985
narrative_blocks.body: After Republicans took the Senate in 1980, he chaired the Foreign Relations Committee through Reagan's first term. He hosted the 16th Karmapa of Tibet at a Capitol luncheon in 1980 and was repeatedly mentioned as a presidential prospect from 1968 onward.

narrative_blocks.header: His daughter Sharon married Jay Rockefeller IV
narrative_blocks.body: His daughter Sharon Percy — twin sister of the murdered Valerie — married John D. 'Jay' Rockefeller IV in 1967, three years before Jay was elected Governor of West Virginia. Sharon would go on to lead WETA-TV in Washington for decades.

narrative_blocks.header: Daughter murdered late in his Senate campaign
narrative_blocks.body: Valerie was beaten and stabbed in her bedroom September 18, 1966; both candidates suspended campaigning two weeks. Police chased 1,317 leads but the case remains unsolved.

narrative_blocks.header: Tearful TV plea saved his Senate seat
narrative_blocks.body: Days before the 1978 election he told Illinois voters: 'I got your message and you're right.' He won 53-46 against an unknown challenger.

cross_connections.display_label: the senator-governor who married his daughter Sharon

bio.extra_names: First wife of Charles H. Percy. Mother of Sharon Percy Rockefeller. Daughter Valerie's name carried Jeanne's middle name.

death.note: Died 1947 when daughters Sharon + Valerie were 3 years old + son Roger was 1.

marriages.notes: m.1943 during Charles's WWII Navy service. Three biological children: twin daughters Valerie + Sharon (b.1944) + son Roger (b.1946). Only Sharon (X02327) built per Hooker scope. Jeanne d.1947 when daughters were 3 + Roger was 1. Daughter Valerie was murdered Sept 18, 1966 age 21 in unsolved cold case (see Charles X02328 NB#1 + Sharon X02327 NB#1).

INST104.name: Asia Society
INST104.notes: Founded 1956 by John D. Rockefeller III (X02324) to foster greater cooperation between Asia and the United States. Major Hooker tree connections: founded by JDR III (Blanchette Hooker Rockefeller's husband — HD3284); chaired by Blanchette Rockefeller Hooker (HD3284) as Asian Cultural Council 1980-1990; her son-in-law-in-law Charles Percy was a Bilderberg member; her son Sen. Jay Rockefeller IV is also AS-affiliated; her grandson Charles P. Rockefeller (HD3291) is an Asia Society trustee; great-grandson Charles delivered the keynote at Asia Society Japan Center launch. Connected institutions: Asian Cultural Council (continuation of JDR III Fund's Asian Cultural Program created 1967), International House of Japan.
